title: wire:intersect
description: Livewire's wire:intersect directive allows you to execute an action when an element enters or leaves the viewport. This is useful for lazy loading content, triggering analytics, or creating scroll-based interactions.

sections.title: #Basic usage
sections.content:
The simplest form runs an action when an element becomes visible:
When the element enters the viewport, the loadMore action will be called on your component.

sections.title: #Enter and leave events
sections.content: You can specify whether to run the action on enter, leave, or both:

sections.title: #Fire once
sections.content:
Use the .once modifier to ensure the action only fires on the first intersection:
This is particularly useful for analytics or tracking when you only want to record the first time a user sees something.

sections.title: #Combining modifiers
sections.content: You can combine multiple modifiers to create precise behaviors:

sections.title: #Comparison with Alpine's x-intersect
sections.content: If you're familiar with Alpine.js, wire:intersect works similarly to x-intersect but triggers Livewire actions instead of Alpine expressions. The modifiers and behavior are designed to feel familiar to Alpine users.
